action.reasoning: Observation: iter3 command failed due to state_dict mismatch (old best.pt from no-attention model has incompatible LSTM/encoder shapes; new attention model uses input_size=768 vs 256). Debug ran partially (vocab=134 printed), loss curve shows training progress (~4.9->1.8) but no new eval yet. Analysis: checkpoint from iter1 is now invalid after arch upgrade; train.py and model.py are truncated/incomplete in workspace (MathExprRecognizer cut off, train save logic missing). Root cause is sequential model evolution without retraining from scratch. Decision: provide complete fixed model.py (add full MathExprRecognizer wrapper + minor fixes for init/forward consistency), complete train.py (add saving logic, increase to 8 epochs for better convergence given ~25s/epoch timing, log exprate), update debug.py to use same params; run train.py && debug.py to train new model from scratch, inspect preds, then submit_eval on updated best.pt for new baseline exprate and time measurement.
